raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need command: find /testbed -type f -name "*.txt" -exec du -b {} + | awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum}'? But need per file and prepend hostname. Use hostname command. So: find /testbed -type f -name "*.txt" -exec du -b {} + | awk -v h="$(hostname)" '{print h, $0}'. But need total per file? Actually "total disk usage for each .txt file" means size per file. So du -b gives size. Prepend hostname. So: find /testbed -type f -name "*.txt" -exec du -b {} + | awk -v h="$(hostname)" '{print h, $0}'. Let's output.
